description_long: This object is a concrete jersey barrier, commonly used for traffic control and safety in urban environments. Its surface is predominantly gray with visible weathering, stains, and patches of rough texture, indicating exposure to outdoor elements. Both of its long faces feature diagonal yellow stripes, which serve as high-visibility markings for drivers and pedestrians. The barrier has a trapezoidal cross-section, with a wide, stable base tapering to a narrower top, and the sides are slightly sloped. The ends are flat and show some minor surface irregularities, consistent with cast concrete. There are no visible handles, holes, or interactive components, and the object appears solid and heavy.
description: A weathered gray concrete jersey barrier with yellow diagonal stripes and a trapezoidal profile, showing signs of outdoor use.
description_view_0: Front face of the jersey barrier with yellow diagonal stripes and visible surface wear.
description_view_1: Narrow end view showing the trapezoidal cross-section and sloped sides.
description_view_2: Opposite long face, also featuring yellow diagonal stripes and weathered concrete texture.
description_view_3: Other narrow end view, similar to view 1, highlighting the thickness and sloped profile.
